county_metadata.record_losses: No significant record losses. Good preservation at county clerk.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Washington County in 1788. Northernmost county on Canadian border at Lake Champlain. Plattsburgh was site of pivotal 1814 Battle of Plattsburgh. Home to SUNY Plattsburgh. Clinton State Prison (Dannemora).
county_metadata.research_tips: Clinton County Clerk in Plattsburgh holds deed records from 1788. Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Location on the Canadian border means significant cross-border family connections with Quebec. Check Quebec parish registers for French Canadian families who settled in Plattsburgh and Champlain areas. The Clinton County Historical Association in Plattsburgh has genealogical files and War of 1812 battle records. The Battle of Plattsburgh (1814) military records at NARA document local militia and regular army participants. SUNY Plattsburgh Special Collections has regional manuscripts. French Canadian immigration records are crucial; St. Peter's Church Plattsburgh (Catholic, from 1830s) has records of many Quebec immigrant families. The Adirondack region portion of the county has lumber industry records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1788; county clerk Plattsburgh

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1788.
Military records from War of 1812 including Battle of Plattsburgh. Local militia and navy records.
Federal census from 1790. Important for tracking French Canadian immigration patterns.
Catholic church records crucial for French Canadian immigrants. St. Peter's Plattsburgh from 1830s.
Plattsburgh Press-Republican, Plattsburgh Sentinel.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Excellent record preservation from formation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Albany County in 1786. Hudson River county. Hudson was a whaling port and early industrial city. Home to Livingston family estates. Martin Van Buren born in Kinderhook.
county_metadata.research_tips: Columbia County Clerk in Hudson holds deed records from 1786. Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1786 records at Albany County. Columbia County was dominated by the Livingston family and their tenant farming system (Livingston Manor). Anti-rent wars (1839-1845) records document tenant farmer resistance. The Columbia County Historical Society in Kinderhook has extensive genealogical collections and Livingston family papers. Martin Van Buren National Historic Site in Kinderhook has Van Buren family records. Hudson's whaling heritage (1780s-1840s) means maritime records are at Columbia County Historical Society. Dutch Reformed church records in Kinderhook, Claverack, and Germantown are essential for colonial families. The Shaker community at Mount Lebanon (1787-1947) generated unique communal records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1786; county clerk Hudson

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Livingston Manor tenant leases important.
Wills, administrations from 1786. Livingston estate records notable.
Dutch Reformed records from Kinderhook, Claverack, Germantown. Essential for colonial families.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival. Holland Society published key records.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1790. Useful for tracking tenancy patterns.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Onondaga County in 1808. Central NY hill country. SUNY Cortland located here. Agricultural and light manufacturing economy.
county_metadata.research_tips: Cortland County Clerk holds deed records from 1808 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. For pre-1808 records check Onondaga County. The Cortland County Historical Society on Maple Avenue maintains genealogical files, cemetery records, and local histories. Most early settlers were New England migrants from Connecticut and Massachusetts who followed the Tully Valley route. The 1808 History of Cortland County by Smith is a key reference. SUNY Cortland's Memorial Library has local history collections. Homer village was the original county seat and its records predate those in Cortland. For early land patents check the NY State Archives Military Tract records, as Cortland County was largely within the Military Tract granted to Revolutionary War veterans.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1808; county clerk Cortland

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Otsego and Ulster Counties in 1797. Catskill Mountains region. Delaware River watershed. Anti-rent movement was strong here. Rural agricultural county with dairy farming.
county_metadata.research_tips: Delaware County Clerk in Delhi holds deed records from 1797 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. For pre-1797 records check both Otsego and Ulster Counties depending on town location. The Delaware County Historical Association in Delhi has extensive genealogical collections including family files, cemetery transcriptions, and farm account books. The anti-rent wars (1840s-1850s) significantly affected land tenure here and court records document tenant farmer disputes with landlords. Many settlers came from Connecticut via the Catskill Turnpike. The county's dairy farming heritage generated rich agricultural society records. Andes, Margaretville, and Walton libraries have local history collections. For the watershed communities that were displaced by NYC reservoir construction (Pepacton, Cannonsville), check Delaware County Historical Association displacement records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1797; county clerk Delhi

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1797.
Federal census from 1800. Agricultural schedules valuable for dairy farming documentation.
Court records from anti-rent wars documenting tenant farmer resistance.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good survival at county and state level.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Birth and death from statewide registration.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant losses. Excellent colonial-era record preservation.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: One of the original 12 counties (1683). Named for Duchess of York. Hudson River county with major estates (Vanderbilt, Roosevelt). Poughkeepsie was temporary state capital. FDR born in Hyde Park.
county_metadata.research_tips: Dutchess County Clerk in Poughkeepsie holds deed records from 1718 (earliest surviving) and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same era. As an original county, Dutchess has some of the oldest records in NY. The Dutchess County Historical Society has extensive genealogical collections. The FDR Presidential Library in Hyde Park has Roosevelt family papers spanning centuries. Adriance Memorial Library in Poughkeepsie has local history room with city directories and family files. Dutch Reformed church records in Fishkill, Poughkeepsie, and Rhinebeck are critical for colonial families. The Palatine German settlement in Rhinebeck (1710s+) is documented in church records and in the Palatines to America society publications. Vassar College Special Collections has regional manuscripts. The county's great estates (Livingston, Beekman, Philipse) generated extensive tenant records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1718; excellent colonial era holdings

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from colonial era. Estate tenant records significant.
Wills, inventories, administrations from colonial era.
Dutch Reformed records from Fishkill, Poughkeepsie, Rhinebeck. Published by Holland Society.
Federal census from 1790. NY state census provides additional detail.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Records of Palatine German immigrants who settled in Rhinebeck and surrounding areas from 1710.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Good through published research and church records.

county_metadata.record_losses: No significant courthouse fires. Records well preserved.
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Niagara County in 1821. Buffalo is second-largest city in NY and major Great Lakes port. Erie Canal terminus (1825). Heavy industry and immigration. Pan-American Exposition (1901).
county_metadata.research_tips: Erie County Clerk in Buffalo holds deed records from 1821 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Buffalo was the western terminus of the Erie Canal (1825) and became a massive immigration gateway. The Buffalo History Museum has extensive genealogical collections including city directories from 1828 and immigrant community records. For German, Irish, Italian, and Polish immigrant research, the Buffalo and Erie County Public Library has an outstanding genealogy department with naturalization indexes, passenger list transcripts, and ethnic organization records. The Diocese of Buffalo Archives has Catholic parish records crucial for immigrant families. For African American genealogy, the Michigan Avenue Baptist Church records (from 1836) document one of the oldest Black congregations in western NY. The Holland Land Company records (NYS Archives) cover early settlement grants in Erie County.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1821; county clerk Buffalo

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Erie Canal-era property transfers significant.
Wills, administrations from 1821.
Massive naturalization records from Buffalo courts. German, Irish, Italian, Polish immigrants.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Excellent. Indexed by Buffalo History Museum.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1830. Manufacturing schedules for Buffalo industry.

Buffalo Courier, Buffalo News, Express. Extensive immigrant press in German, Italian, Polish.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Clinton County in 1799. Adirondack Mountains county. Contains highest peaks in NY. Iron mining industry. Lake Placid (1932 and 1980 Olympics). Crown Point and Fort Ticonderoga.
county_metadata.research_tips: Essex County Clerk in Elizabethtown holds deed records from 1799 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1799 records in Clinton County. This Adirondack county has sparse settlement records before the 1800s. The Essex County Historical Society in Elizabethtown has genealogical files and iron mining industry records. The Adirondack History Museum in Elizabethtown documents lumber and iron mining families. Fort Ticonderoga has colonial and Revolutionary War records relevant to Essex County families. Lake Placid Public Library has Olympic-era and resort community records. The iron mining communities (Port Henry, Moriah, Mineville) generated employment records through the Witherbee Sherman company. Many settlers came from Vermont across Lake Champlain. Check Vermont records for families appearing in Essex County after 1800.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1799; county clerk Elizabethtown

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1799.
Colonial and Revolutionary War records. Garrison lists, court martial records, military correspondence.
Federal census from 1800. Useful for tracking iron mining populations.
Birth and death from statewide registration.
Essex County Republican, Ticonderoga Sentinel.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Clinton County in 1808. Northern Adirondack county on Canadian border. Malone is county seat. Lumber, mining, and dairy farming. Contains part of St. Regis Mohawk Reservation.
county_metadata.research_tips: Franklin County Clerk in Malone holds deed records from 1808 and Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1808 records in Clinton County. The county shares the Canadian border and St. Regis Mohawk Reservation (Akwesasne) straddles the US-Canada border. Mohawk Nation records are maintained by the tribe and are separate from county records. The Franklin County Historical and Museum Society in Malone has genealogical files. French Canadian immigration was significant — check Quebec parish registers for families settling in the Malone and Chateaugay areas. The lumber industry dominated the economy and generated camp records and employment lists. Malone was birthplace of William Almon Wheeler (VP under Hayes). The Adirondack Correctional Facility and other state institutions generated employee records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1808; county clerk Malone

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation.
Wills, administrations from 1808.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Created from Montgomery County in 1838. Southern Adirondack foothills. Johnstown was original Tryon/Montgomery county seat. Glove-making capital of US. Sacandaga Reservoir (Great Sacandaga Lake) displaced communities.
county_metadata.research_tips: Fulton County Clerk in Johnstown holds deed records from 1838. Surrogate's Court has probate from the same year. Pre-1838 records are in Montgomery County. Johnstown has deeper history as the seat of old Tryon County (1772-1784) and Montgomery County (1784-1838), so many regional records are here even for neighboring counties. The Fulton County Historical Society maintains genealogical files. The glove-making industry (Gloversville and Johnstown) employed thousands of immigrant workers, especially from southern Italy — check Italian church records at St. Mary's and local labor records. The Sacandaga Reservoir (1930) displaced several communities and their records were scattered. Check the Sacandaga Valley historical group for displaced family records. Sir William Johnson's papers (colonial superintendent of Indian affairs) are at NYS Library and cover this region extensively.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Deed records from 1838; county clerk Johnstown

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed and mortgage records from formation. Pre-1838 at Montgomery County.
Wills, administrations from 1838.

source_profiles.survival_notes: Partial. Church and fraternal organization records survive.
